components.label: Four-segment consolidated healthcare franchise (devices, diagnostics, nutrition, EPD)
components.ownership_claim: Consolidated owner-cash franchise across devices, diagnostics, nutrition, and EPD

low.label: FY2025 net cash from operating activities
FY2025 payments to acquire productive assets
Normalized owner cash per diluted share (OCF minus capital spending)
low.rationale: FY2025 owner cash per share anchors the four-segment consolidated franchise; GAAP net income is not run-rate after FY2024 one-time items.

low.label: Duration-adjusted owner-cash capitalization multiple
low.rationale: Bear stresses CGM competition and nutrition pricing; base matches Lawrence seven-year scenario envelope; bull modest Libre and structural-heart acceleration.

components.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Low/base/high reflect CGM competition, nutrition pricing, and device growth scenarios.
components.falsifier: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or margin path is materially worse than low case.

components.label: Near-term surplus cash and balance-sheet claims
components.ownership_claim: Near-term surplus cash claim not capitalized in core franchise terminal

low.label: Filing-locked net cash per share (cash less long-term debt)
Near-term surplus cash claim not capitalized in core franchise terminal
low.rationale: Low case reserves excess cash for debt service; base credits modest surplus above operating working-capital needs; high case adds bounded excess liquidity after FY2025 debt paydown. Not a second subtraction of full net debt.

base.label: Filing-locked net cash per share (cash less long-term debt)
Near-term surplus cash claim not capitalized in core franchise terminal
base.rationale: Low case reserves excess cash for debt service; base credits modest surplus above operating working-capital needs; high case adds bounded excess liquidity after FY2025 debt paydown. Not a second subtraction of full net debt.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

top_value_drivers.label: Four-segment consolidated healthcare franchise (devices, diagnostics, nutrition, EPD)
Margin compression, CGM competition, and nutrition pricing reserve
Near-term surplus cash and balance-sheet claims

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: The current component schedule is the first decision-grade baseline; attribution begins with the next dated valuation snapshot.
